title: Healey, Campbell launch portal for reporting ICE misconduct
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Gov. Maura Healey and Attorney General Andrea Campbell launched a portal for reporting alleged ICE misconduct in Massachusetts.
The portal allows residents to submit reports, photos, and videos of possible unlawful activity by federal immigration agents.
Campbell's office updated a 'know your rights' guide, covering protests, CBP roles, and executive orders on ICE accountability.

title: Jaylen Brown criticizes NBA officials for allowing foul-baiting, rewarding flopping
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jaylen Brown criticizes NBA officials for encouraging foul-baiting and rewarding flopping tactics during games.
Brown expressed frustration following the Celtics' loss to Oklahoma City, highlighting the impact of officiating on game outcomes.
Despite fouling concerns, Brown scored 34 points and played 41 minutes in the game against the Thunder.

title: Oscars 2026 predictions: Full list of who will win each category
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: "One Battle After Another" is the frontrunner for Best Picture at the 2026 Oscars, but "Sinners" remains a strong contender.
Michael B. Jordan is predicted to win Best Actor for "Sinners," with Ethan Hawke being the critic's choice for his role in "Blue Moon."
Paul Thomas Anderson is expected to win Best Director for "One Battle After Another," following wins from major awards bodies.

title: Why new Patriots safety Kevin Byard predicted New England’s breakthrough season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Kevin Byard joins the New England Patriots on a one-year, $9 million contract as a veteran safety.
Byard reunites with coach Mike Vrabel, crediting his leadership for the team's recent success.
Byard aims to strengthen the Patriots' defense, adding to a secondary with rising players like Craig Woodson.

title: ‘Our small way of saying thank you’: A mother-son duo is running the Boston Marathon for MGH
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Lisa and Benjamin Levine from Needham are running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support Massachusetts General Hospital (MGH).
The Levines' family has been connected to MGH for over 25 years, with significant personal and professional ties.
Their marathon participation is a gesture of gratitude for MGH's lifesaving care, including during critical family health crises.

title: $25M winning lottery ticket sold at Turners Falls convenience store
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Open Door Trust of Boston won $25 million in the Massachusetts State Lottery's 'Mega Money' game.
Trustee Renat Lumpau accepted a one-time payment of $16.25 million before taxes for the winning ticket.
Cumberland Farms in Turners Falls sold the winning ticket, earning a $50,000 bonus.

title: After prior uproar over removal, new mayor to restore Italian flag road lines to Newton
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Mayor Marc Laredo plans to restore Italian flag colors to Adams Street in Newton, removed last year over safety concerns.
The removal of the tricolor stripe last summer was due to traffic safety requirements and caused controversy among residents.
Designated parking lanes on Adams Street now allow for the return of the red, white, and green road markings.

title: Student charged in Danvers murder allegedly told police he ‘wanted to kill someone for a long time’
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Anthony DeMayo, 18, charged with murder and home invasion in Janet Swallow's death in Danvers.
DeMayo allegedly confessed, stating he'd wanted to kill someone for a long time, after random attack.
Police arrested DeMayo carrying a knife with blood, and he's undergoing psychiatric evaluation.

title: Revolution announce return of original homegrown player Diego Fagundez on 1-year contract
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Diego Fagundez returns to New England Revolution on a one-year contract with a club option for 2027.
Fagundez, 31, began his MLS career with the Revolution at age 16 and is an MLS veteran with 16 seasons.
Revolution's first homegrown player, Fagundez, adds experience to the team after stints with Austin FC and LA Galaxy.

title: Chunks of Storrow Drive and Soldiers Field Road to be closed Sunday night
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Storrow Drive and Soldiers Field Road will be closed from 9 p.m. Sunday to 5 a.m. Monday for tunnel maintenance.
Detours will reroute traffic from North Harvard Street to Memorial Drive, returning via the Longfellow Bridge in Cambridge.
Closure coincides with the South Boston St. Patrick's Day parade; MBTA Red Line offers extended service from 10 a.m. to 6 p.m.
